bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages, I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0 and no evidence of the opponent’s priorities yet, I will start by asking for my top value on every issue.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself while making only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent’s only offer was all valueA, which is extremely poor for my preferences and shows no meaningful concession yet, so I will hold firm on the high-importance issues C and D and on issueB.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid, conceding only on low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issues C and D. Since it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer with no concession, there is little reason to make a major move yet, but I avoid simply repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high-utility offer because it is still very early (t=0.06), and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering my lowest-value bid on every issue. I will continue conceding only on my lowest-weight issue, issueA, while holding firm on the more important issues C and D and moderately important issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility early bid by conceding only on the lowest-weight issueA while holding my important issuesB, issueC, and issueD at valueF. Time is still very early, and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so there is no reason yet to make larger concessions.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.82 rather than making major concessions. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA with no concession, so I will concede fully on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small additional concession because it is still early at t=0.12. The opponent has held firm at all valueA with no concession, so I should not give up high-weight issues substantially yet; I already conceded issueA fully, and now concede slightly on issueD from valueF to valueE because that costs me relatively little.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.75 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering the all-valueA bid. Since issueA is already fully conceded and issueB is lower weight than issueC/issueD, I make a modest additional concession on issueB while preserving high value on the most important issues as much as possible.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around the mid-0.7 range, since time is only 0.16 and there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer with no concession, so I should avoid rewarding that by moving too far; I make only a small additional concession from my last bid by moving issueC from valueF to valueC while holding strong on issueD and keeping issueB at valueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the low 0.70s because it is early at t=0.18 and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the worst valid values for me. Since I have already conceded fully on low-weight issueA, I will keep high-weight issueD strong while making a modest change on issueC to avoid repeating my last bid and continue gradual movement.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69, since time is only at 0.20 and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the all-valueA bid. I made a modest concession from my last bid by moving issueD from valueF to valueE, which is a small utility loss for me, while still protecting higher-importance issues C and D reasonably well.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69, which is a meaningful concession from my opening but still protects my most important issues. At t=0.22 there is still time, and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, which is only about 0.18 utility to me. With no messages and no movement from them, I will avoid collapsing further and instead make a different tradeoff while holding strong on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.68, since it is only t=0.24 and there is no evidence the opponent is conceding: every opponent bid has stayed at all valueA, which is very poor for me. I have already conceded fully on low-weight issueA, and I am making only a small non-repeating adjustment on issueB while holding the high-weight issues C and D at favorable values.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.62 by conceding further on lower-weight issueB while protecting the high-weight issueC and issueD values. Time is still relatively early at t=0.26, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s all-valueA position yet, especially since their offers show no concession at all and no messages provide additional credible signal.

I am targeting a moderate utility around the low 0.6 range because it is still early-mid negotiation, so I should not move all the way to the opponent’s all-valueA demand yet. The opponent has not conceded at all and has repeatedly offered the same all-valueA bid, so I am making a small additional concession on a high-importance issue while still preserving value on issueC and issueD. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

At t=0.30 I am still not near the deadline, so I target a moderate utility concession rather than dropping to the opponent’s all-valueA bid, which is only worth about 0.18 to me. The opponent has repeated the exact same all-valueA offer with no concession or messages, so I will continue conceding mainly on already-weakened issues while preserving some value on the high-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.52, conceding further from my last offer while still protecting value on the high-weight issueC. At t=0.32 there is still time, but the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA; with no messages to assess, I am making a controlled concession rather than moving close to their demand.

I targeted a utility just under my previous offer (about 0.48), since we are still before the midpoint but should continue making measured concessions. The opponent has repeatedly offered only the all-valueA bid and has shown no concession, so I am holding some value on high-weight issues C and D while already conceding fully on lower/medium issues A and B.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.50 rather than collapsing toward the opponent’s all-valueA demand, since we are only at t=0.36 and there is still time to negotiate. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer with no concession or useful messages, so I will avoid rewarding that rigidity too much while still varying my prior bid and keeping concessions mainly away from the highest-value combinations.

I am targeting a moderate utility around the low-0.49 range, since we are still before the midpoint and the opponent has not shown any concession beyond repeating the all-valueA bid. I have already conceded fully on lower/medium issues A and B, so this offer varies the high-weight C/D tradeoff without immediately collapsing to the opponent’s very low-utility proposal for me. There are no opponent messages, and the offer history suggests they are holding firm rather than reciprocating concessions.

I targeted a utility around 0.45: still well above the reservation value, but meaningfully more concessive than my early offers. At t=0.40 there is moderate time pressure, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA; with no messages to interpret, I am making a controlled concession while avoiding their full low-utility bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.44, making only a small concession from my previous offer because we are still in the mid-game rather than near the deadline. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA with no concession and no messages, so I am not treating their position as informative beyond strong firmness; I preserve some value on the high-weight issues while still moving slightly closer.

I targeted a reduced but still positive utility around 0.42 by already conceding fully on the lower-weight issues A and B, while retaining modest value on the high-weight issues C and D. Time is mid-negotiation, so I am making a real concession from my last bid but not collapsing to the opponent’s repeated all-valueA position yet. The opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so I am moving cautiously while testing whether they will accept partial movement on C and D.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.42 by conceding three issues to the opponent’s repeatedly signaled valueA while preserving one high-importance issue for myself. Time is near the mid-point, so I should make a meaningful concession, but the opponent has shown no concession at all and has only offered the all-valueA bid, which is still too low for me.

I am targeting a still-positive mid-game utility around 0.405, conceding slightly from my last bid while preserving value on the important issueD. The opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so I should not rush to their position at t=0.48 but should continue gradual movement with a non-repeated valid bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility while preserving value on the high-weight issues C and D, since the opponent has shown no concession at all and keeps repeating the all-valueA bid. At time 0.50 there is still room to avoid collapsing to their demand, but I continue to concede on lower-weight issues A and B and offer a tradeoff on C/D rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a mid-level utility around 0.55: enough concession for t=0.52, but still well above the opponent’s repeated all-valueA bid. The opponent has not conceded at all and sent no messages, so I am conceding mainly on issueA and issueB, while preserving value on the high-weight issueD and giving some ground on issueC.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.50, continuing to concede because we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA and sending no messages, so I am moving closer to their apparent position while still preserving value on the high-importance issueC/issueD tradeoff and avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the mid-game rather than conceding all the way to the opponent’s repeated all-valueA demand, since they have shown no concession at all through t=0.56. I have already conceded heavily on issueA and issueB, so I will keep some value on the higher-weight issues issueC and issueD while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer.

I am targeting a utility around 0.48, preserving some value on the high-weight issueC and issueD while already conceding fully on issueA and issueB. At t=0.58 there is meaningful time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s unchanged all-valueA bid, which gives me very low utility; the opponent has shown no concession and sent no messages, so I am making a further moderate concession without collapsing to their position.

I am targeting a utility around 0.40 by conceding three issues to the opponent’s repeatedly demanded valueA while preserving valueC on issueC, one of my highest-weight issues. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to accept the opponent’s all-valueA bid, which only gives me very low utility. The opponent has shown no concession at all and sent no messages, so I am making a clear but controlled concession without rewarding total rigidity yet.

I am targeting a reduced utility around 0.324, which is still above the all-valueA outcome but makes a meaningful concession from my last offer. The opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so at t=0.62 I am moving closer to their apparent demand while preserving some value on issueC rather than immediately collapsing to their full offer.

I am targeting a modest utility above reservation while preserving value on the high-weight issueD; at t=0.64 there is time pressure, but not enough to concede fully to the opponent’s repeated all-valueA bid. The opponent has shown no concession at all, so I am moving closer to their pattern by setting issueA, issueB, and issueC to valueA while keeping only issueD above their demand.

I am targeting a modest utility around 0.32 by keeping only a small concession point on issueC while giving the opponent valueA on the other issues. At t=0.66 the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so I am moving closer to that pattern but avoiding a full collapse to their exact bid yet.

I am targeting a modest utility of about 0.399 by keeping the opponent’s apparent priorities mostly intact while asking for one small improvement on issueC. At t=0.68 there is time pressure, but not enough to simply collapse to the opponent’s latest bid; their first real concession was moving issueC to valueB, so I will test whether they can move one step further on that same issue. There are no messages, and the offer history shows they held firm on valueA for most issues.

At t=0.70, the opponent has been extremely firm on valueA for all issues, with only a small recent concession to issueC: valueB. Since deadline pressure is now significant and their last offer still gives me positive utility around 0.324, I am matching their signaled acceptable bid to maximize the chance of agreement rather than risking no deal.
